title: Denial of Service Attack
text: Denial of Service Attack A denial of service attack attempts to make a computer resource unavailable to its intended users. A common type of attack is one in which so many logon attempts or page accesses are generated that significant CPU time is consumed and legitimate users cannot logon or experience degraded performance. Other types of attack include sending requests with large data content. Denial of service attacks can focus on other bottlenecks and weaknesses in the application. Network traffic can also be a potential denial of service target. Defense Only allow authenticated and authorized users to use large pages. Minimize the total size of unauthenticated pages. Configure the Uniface Web Application Server to limit the size of requests. Use the servlet setting MAXPOSTDATASIZE . For more information, see MAXPOSTDATASIZE . Stress test your Uniface application to find out where the bottlenecks are, and take appropriate action. For more information, see Performance and Scalability . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
